dependencies: Natural gas fueling infrastructure not yet broadly available in U.S. and Canada
Dependence on third-party disposal facilities and transfer stations
Supply chain constraints on truck deliveries
risks: Labor shortages and external strikes impacting operations
Supply chain constraints reducing availability of certain assets
Increased costs for wage adjustments and training due to constrained labor market
Geopolitical conflicts leading to volatility in commodity prices and vendor disruptions
Significant new restrictions and tariffs on foreign trade could increase cost of equipment procured from outside U.S.
Shortages in diesel fuel supply or increases in diesel fuel prices
Market disruption from port strikes negatively impacting recycling commodity prices

regulatory_risks: Extensive federal, state, provincial and local environmental regulations (RCRA, CERCLA, Clean Water Act, Clean Air Act)
Changes in regulations regarding emerging contaminants such as PFAS could result in increased compliance costs
Extended producer responsibility (EPR) regulations could significantly impact waste and recycling streams
Regulations requiring diversion of organic wastes away from landfills
State and local governments mandating recycling and waste reduction, prohibiting disposal of certain materials
Flow control regulations requiring waste be deposited at specific sites
Fitness laws allowing agencies to deny or revoke contracts based on compliance history
Medical waste regulations varying by state with lack of uniformity
Controlled Substances Act compliance requirements for collection and disposal of controlled substances
EPA focus on landfill emissions standards and methane measurement technologies
California Climate Corporate Data Accountability Act requiring Scope 1, 2, and 3 GHG emissions disclosure
SEC climate-related disclosure rules (currently stayed pending judicial review)
European Union Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD) requiring extensive sustainability disclosures
Regulations mandating transition to electric powered vehicles could impair natural gas fleet investments
Changes to Renewable Fuel Standard (RFS) program could impact RNG business and RINs market volatility
New U.S. presidential administration may reduce existing renewable fuel targets or increase small refinery exemptions
Environmental justice requirements in permitting decisions
Privacy and data protection laws (U.S. and international) related to secure information destruction services
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and anti-corruption laws compliance
Potential changes to tax laws including expiration of alternative fuel tax credits
Inflation Reduction Act provisions creating uncertainty around tax credits and incentives

sanction_risks: New U.S. presidential administration calling for substantial changes to foreign trade policy
Significant new restrictions and tariffs on foreign trade could negatively impact recycling export business and cross-border commerce with Canada
Geopolitical conflicts and international responses may lead to volatility in commodity prices and vendor disruptions
